ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general pizza food product class (FOODON_00002681); no specific pepperoni pizza subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a category; no pepperoni-specific subcode exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226559009 covers pizza generically; no pepperoni pizza-specific code exists. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one slice of a standard 12-inch pepperoni pizza, weighing approximately 107 g.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza, pepperoni, regular crust — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 21299)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for pepperoni pizza, regular crust, used as primary nutrient reference. Values represent per-100g basis derived from the USDA FoodData Central database entry for pepperoni pizza.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal); University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 60 is based on published values for regular-crust pizza with cheese and tomato toppings from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283. Pepperoni pizza is nutritionally analogous to cheese pizza with meat topping; GI is driven primarily by the refined wheat crust. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 × 33.2) / 100 ≈ 19.9, using 107 g serving size and 31.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (640 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit pepperoni pizza consumption due to its high sodium content (640 mg/100 g), as regular intake can contribute significantly to exceeding the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg and elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 60) and moderate carbohydrate load from refined wheat crust
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume pepperoni pizza in controlled portions, as its medium GI (approximately 60) and refined carbohydrate content from the wheat crust can cause moderate postprandial blood glucose elevation; pairing with a salad and limiting to one slice may help manage glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cheese and pepperoni, high sodium, and processed meat content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should restrict pepperoni pizza intake due to its combination of saturated fat (4.0 g/100 g), high sodium (640 mg/100 g), and processed meat (pepperoni), all of which are associated with increased cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pepperoni pizza is a widely popular Italian-American baked flatbread dish consisting of a yeasted wheat dough base topped with tomato sauce, melted mozzarella cheese, and slices of pepperoni — a spiced, cured pork-and-beef sausage with a characteristic smoky, mildly spicy flavour. The pizza is baked at high temperature until the crust is golden and crisp at the edges, the cheese is melted and lightly browned, and the pepperoni edges curl and crisp. The flavour profile is savoury, umami-rich, and slightly spicy, with a contrast between the chewy or crisp crust and the soft, melted cheese. Nutritionally, pepperoni pizza provides moderate energy (approximately 266 kcal per 100 g), meaningful protein (11.4 g/100 g) from cheese and meat, and significant carbohydrate (31 g/100 g) from the refined wheat crust. It is notably high in sodium (640 mg/100 g) due to the cured pepperoni and cheese, and contains saturated fat (4.0 g/100 g). The medium glycemic index (approximately 60) means it can cause moderate postprandial blood glucose rises, making portion control important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The high sodium and saturated fat content make it a food of concern for those managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. In Singapore, pepperoni pizza is served at Turkish and Lebanese restaurants such as Sultan Palace, reflecting the global adoption of pizza across diverse culinary settings. It is classified as a processed mixed dish and is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those requiring halal or kosher certification without specific product verification.
common_user_queries: How many calories in pepperoni pizza?
How much sodium is in pepperoni pizza?
Is pepperoni pizza high in fat?
What is the glycemic index of pizza?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for pepperoni pizza (regular crust), which is an exact match for this food type. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature for analogous pizza types. All mandatory fields are complete.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not consistently reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for pepperoni pizza and is not reliably quantified in this food category. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the primary USDA SR Legacy source for this food. The cuisine tag in the input JSON lists 'Turkish' reflecting the restaurant context (Sultan Palace, a Turkish and Lebanese restaurant in Singapore); however, pepperoni pizza is canonically an Italian-American dish and has been classified accordingly. The GI value of 60 is an analogous estimate based on published GI values for regular-crust cheese pizza from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008); no direct GI measurement for pepperoni pizza was located in the approved sources. Halal status is listed as false because conventional pepperoni contains pork; halal-certified pepperoni variants exist but cannot be confirmed for this specific restaurant offering without product-level verification.
